blocks.text: Amanda Golden, VP Finance, Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez

This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} has engaged Fernhollow Partners LLC to conduct a transaction readiness review of Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez. The work begins {{fact:f:E-2022-001.start}} and runs approximately seven months, through delivery of a written readiness assessment and a briefing to your board. We are glad the working relationship continues in your new seat, and we intend to keep earning it.

The purpose of the review is plain: when a buyer or lender arrives, your numbers should already answer the questions. We will assemble and organize the data room, perform a quality-of-numbers review of the financial statements and supporting schedules, and prepare management for diligence itself. Document needs come to you in a single request list at kickoff. We do not opine on legal, tax, or accounting matters, and we do not negotiate on your behalf. The deliverables are an organized data room, a readiness assessment with remediation items ranked by importance, and a short briefing suitable for board use.

Sandra Perez, Director, leads the engagement day to day and is your first call. Briana Dennis, Senior Associate, runs the quality-of-numbers review and drafts the assessment. Ryan Strong, Analyst, builds the data room index and ties the schedules to source. I read the assessment and the remediation log before either reaches you.

Our fee for this scope is {{fact:f:E-2022-001.fee}}. The fee is fixed and covers professional time and routine expenses. Unusual expenses require your written approval before we incur them. Invoices are issued monthly and are payable on receipt. Should the scope change, we agree the adjustment in writing before additional work begins.

We hold your information in confidence. Materials you provide are used only for this engagement and are returned or destroyed at your direction when the work concludes. Our workpapers remain our property.
If these terms are acceptable, please sign below and return one copy to my attention. Until then, questions can go to Sandra Perez or to me directly. We look forward to the work.
